practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate the correct use of a microscope.
Prepare temporary slides of plant and animal cells for observation under a microscope.
Examine and identify plant and animal cells under a microscope.
Calculate the magnification of a specimen.
Investigate the properties of enzymes.
Investigate the effect of temperature and pH on enzyme action (e.g., using catalase or amylase).
Conduct chemical tests for the presence of starch (Iodine test), reducing sugars (Benedict's test), proteins (Biuret test), and lipids (Emulsion/Ethanol test) in food samples.

subtopics: 10.5.1 External and internal structure of a leaf
practical_and_experimental_content: Examine the external and internal structure of a dicotyledonous leaf.
Demonstrate the process of testing a leaf for starch.
Investigate the necessity of light, carbon dioxide, and chlorophyll for photosynthesis.
Investigate the production of oxygen during photosynthesis (e.g., using Elodea/water weed).
Investigate the growth of Rhizopus (bread mould) under different conditions.
Examine the structure of Rhizopus under a microscope or hand lens.
Examine and identify the different types of mammalian teeth.
Investigate the action of amylase on starch.

subtopics: 11.1.1 Internal structure of roots and stems
11.1.2 Absorption of water and uptake of mineral salts
practical_and_experimental_content: Examine the distribution of xylem and phloem in cross-sections of roots and stems.
Demonstrate the process of osmosis using living tissues (e.g., potato osmoscope).
Investigate the movement of water through the stem using a coloured dye.
Investigate the factors that affect the rate of transpiration (using a potometer).
Examine prepared slides of blood smears to identify red and white blood cells.
Examine the structure of a mammalian heart (using a model or specimen dissection).
Investigate the effect of physical activity on pulse rate.
Examine the macroscopic structure of a mammalian kidney.

topics.topic: 11.6 The Nervous System and Sense Organs
practical_and_experimental_content: Investigate simple reflex actions in humans (e.g., knee-jerk reflex, pupil reflex).
Examine the structure of the mammalian eye (using a model or specimen).
Identify the main bones of the mammalian axial and appendicular skeleton.
Examine the structure of a typical freely movable joint.
Demonstrate the action of antagonistic muscles in the arm.
Examine and identify different methods of vegetative propagation (e.g., tubers, runners, corms, bulbs).
Examine the structural parts of a typical flower.
Investigate the conditions necessary for seed germination (water, oxygen, suitable temperature).
Examine the structure of a dicotyledonous seed and a monocotyledonous seed.
Investigate continuous and discontinuous variation in a sample population (e.g., measuring heights or observing tongue rolling).
Use a simple dichotomous key to identify organisms.
Examine and identify the main external features of arthropods (insects, arachnids, crustaceans, diplopods, chilopods).
Investigate the physical composition of different soil samples (sand, loam, clay).
Conduct experiments to compare the drainage and water retention capacities of different soils.
Investigate the presence of air and water in a soil sample.
Investigate a local ecosystem to identify biotic and abiotic factors.
Use sampling techniques (e.g., quadrats) to estimate population size of plant species in a habitat.
